title: Monsoon arrives early in Mizoram, IMD urges public vigilance - News On AIR
article_text:
Monsoon has officially set in over Mizoram today, according to the India Meteorological Department, New Delhi. Officials have urged people across the state to remain alert as the rainy season begins.

The IMD said the monsoon has arrived six days earlier than usual this year in Mizoram, other Northeastern states and Kerala.

IMD Aizawl Director Sudheer Kumar Dwivedi said experts had earlier predicted the onset on the 26th of May. He said the arrival of the monsoon is determined through scientific analysis of wind patterns over the Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal, along with rainfall activity.

Based on these observations, the IMD officially announced the onset of the monsoon.

The IMD has also warned that Mizoram is highly vulnerable to landslides during the rainy season. Residents and vehicle drivers have been advised to remain cautious, especially during heavy rainfall.

title: IMD: Southwest monsoon set to hit India's coasts soon - NewsBytes
article_text:
Kerala, Karnataka to receive heavy rainfall
IMD: Southwest monsoon set to hit India's coasts soon
India
May 26, 2026

The southwest monsoon is almost here. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), it is set to hit parts of the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and Andaman Sea in the next couple of days.
If you are in Kerala, Mahe, or Lakshadweep, expect light to moderate rain with thunderstorms and lightning from May 26-29.
Similar weather is coming for Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Rayalaseema, Coastal Andhra Pradesh, and interior Karnataka and North Interior Karnataka.

Karnataka and Northeast expect heavy storms

South Interior Karnataka could see thundersqualls with winds up to 70 kph from May 26-29, and North Interior Karnataka might get some hailstorms on May 27.
In Assam and Meghalaya, very heavy rain is expected on May 26-27 and will continue as heavy showers through May 31, including Arunachal Pradesh.
Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, and Tripura are also likely to get isolated heavy rainfall on May 26 as well as at the end of the month.
So yes, monsoon season is kicking off with a bang.

title: IMD issues orange alert for heavy rain in Andaman, West Bengal, Sikkim on May 17 - News On AIR
article_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an orange alert for heavy rainfall over Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Sub Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim for tomorrow. Similar conditions will prevail over Keralam, Mahe, Lakshadweep, South Interior Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, Karaikal and Parts of North Eastern Region for tomorrow.

According to IMD, thunderstorms accompanied with lightning and gusty winds over Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Madhya Maharashtra, Marathwada, Odisha and Rayalaseema, Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Konkan, Goa, Telangana, Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand for next two to three days. The weather agency has predicted heat wave conditions in Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh and Vidarbha for tomorrow.
